subtitle: The moving flame of the 1857 rising that refused to die

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of a lone rider against a vast dusty plain at dusk
panels.action: Tatya Tope rides alone across a wind-swept Central Indian plain, cloak and turban streaming as the sun sinks red behind scattered hills and distant ruined ramparts.

dialogue.text: Tatya Tope, general of a broken revolt, turns defeat into a long, burning march across India’s dust-laden heart.

i18n.hi: टूटी बगावत का सरदार तांत्या टोपे, हार को बदल देता है भारत के धूल भरे दिल में जलती हुई यात्रा में।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot from a hilltop overlooking Gwalior fort and city
panels.action: Tatya Tope stands on a rocky rise near Gwalior in mid-1858, looking down towards the distant fort as smoke hangs over the city and scattered rebel encampments break and move.

dialogue.text: In June 1858, Gwalior falls. The great standard of revolt, raised by Rani Lakshmibai and Tatya Tope, is torn down by British columns and loyalist troops.

i18n.hi: जून १८५८, ग्वालियर गिरता है। रानी लक्ष्मीबाई और तांत्या टोपे का फहराया बड़ा बगावत का झंडा, फिरंगियों और वफादार फ़ौजों के हाथों उखाड़ दिया जाता है।

panels.shot: medium close-up three-quarter profile of Tatya Tope, wind in his hair, eyes narrowed
panels.action: Tatya Tope, dusty and tense, grips the hilt of his sword as he turns away from the distant fort, his face lined with fatigue and resolve, a handful of horsemen waiting behind him.

dialogue.text: With Jhansi lost, with Gwalior lost, Tatya’s army scatters. Many leaders flee or sue for mercy. He chooses the road.

i18n.hi: झाँसी गई, ग्वालियर गया, तांत्या की फ़ौज बिखर जाती है। कई सरदार भाग जाते हैं या माफी माँगते हैं। वह सड़क चुनता है।

panels.shot: bird's-eye view of a small column moving through scrub and ravines
panels.action: A small, thin column of horse and a few footmen, led by Tatya Tope, winds along a narrow track between thorny scrub and dry ravines under a harsh Central Indian sun.

dialogue.text: He gathers fragments—Jhansi men, Gwalior cavalry, village matchlock-men. No longer a grand army, but a moving ember, hard to crush.

i18n.hi: वह टुकड़े जुटाता है—झाँसी के जवान, ग्वालियर की घुड़सवार, गाँव के बंदूकची। अब कोई बड़ी फ़ौज नहीं, पर चलती हुई चिंगारी, जिसे रौंदना आसान नहीं।

panels.shot: wide night shot lit by campfires, low-angle from the ground
panels.action: Under a dark sky speckled with stars, Tatya Tope squats near a small campfire with his men in a hidden hollow, horses tethered nearby, maps and a rough sketch of Central India spread on the ground.

dialogue.text: British despatches call him ‘the most indefatigable of the rebels’. Columns are ordered to chase him through Bundelkhand, Malwa and Rajputana.

i18n.hi: अँग्रेज़ी रपटें उसे ‘सबसे थकान न मानने वाला बाग़ी’ कहती हैं। टुकड़ियों को हुक्म है कि उसे बुंदेलखंड, मालवा और राजपूताना भर में दौड़ाओ।

panels.shot: tight overhead shot of Tatya’s hand tracing routes on a rough map drawn in the dirt
panels.action: Tatya Tope’s calloused hand traces lines in the dust, marking rivers and forts, while his weathered face hovers over the impromptu map in flickering firelight.

dialogue.text: From the Chambal to the Narmada, from the forests of Central India to the plains of Rajasthan, he plots paths that dodge roads and cantonments.

i18n.hi: चंबल से नर्मदा तक, मध्य भारत के जंगलों से राजस्थान के मैदानी इलाकों तक, वह ऐसे रास्ते चुनता है जो सड़कों और छावनियों से कतराते हैं।

panels.shot: dynamic mid-shot of Tatya Tope mounting a horse at first light
panels.action: At pale dawn, dew still on the grass, Tatya Tope swings into the saddle, signalling his weary men as they rise and tighten turbans, the camp already being erased.

dialogue.text: He abandons siege and set-piece battle. Speed, surprise and endurance become his weapons. Villages, not forts, are his shelter.

i18n.hi: वह घेराबंदी और सीधी जंग छोड़ देता है। अब उसकी हथियार हैं रफ़्तार, अचानक वार और सहनशक्ति। किले नहीं, गाँव उसकी पनाहगाह हैं।

panels.shot: wide shot of a sudden cavalry rush through a dusty village lane
panels.action: Tatya Tope leads a sudden charge of horsemen into a sleepy roadside village, dust rising, shop shutters half-open as startled sepoys in British service scramble to form up.

dialogue.text: He strikes where garrisons are thin, where treasuries move, where supply lines run. Columns report ‘sudden attacks’, then find only hoofprints in the dust.

i18n.hi: वह वहीं удар करता है जहाँ छावनियाँ कमज़ोर, जहाँ ख़ज़ाने चलते हैं, जहाँ रसद के रस्ते जाते हैं। फ़ौजी लिखते हैं ‘अचानक हमला’, और फिर बस धूल में खुरों के निशान मिलते हैं।

panels.shot: medium shot inside a village courtyard, women and elders with Tatya Tope
panels.action: In a shaded courtyard, Tatya Tope speaks quietly with village elders and women, his hands folded respectfully, as youths stand at the gateway keeping watch.

dialogue.text: British reports call him plunderer; village memory remembers a leader who paid for grain, spared homes, and spoke of dharma and lost kingdoms.

i18n.hi: फिरंगी रपटें उसे लुटेरा कहती हैं; गाँव की याद उसे वो नेता मानती है जो अनाज का दाम देता, घरों को बख्शता, और धरम व खोए हुए राजों की बात करता था।

panels.shot: wide desert-like landscape in Rajputana with distant fort on a hill
panels.action: Tatya Tope rides with a small escort across a dry, stony plain of Rajputana, a distant Rajput fort silhouetted on a hill, while heat haze shimmers around them.

dialogue.text: By late 1858, Forrest’s records follow him westward into Rajputana—towards Tonk, Jhalrapatan and the fringes of the desert, always just beyond the British net.

i18n.hi: १८५८ के अंत तक, फ़ॉरेस्ट की नोंदें उसे राजपूताना की ओर पीछा करती हैं—टोंक, झालरापाटन और रेगिस्तान के किनारों की तरफ, हमेशा अंग्रेज़ी जाल से थोड़ी दूर।

panels.shot: split-depth shot, foreground Tatya, background distant British column
panels.action: From behind a rocky outcrop, Tatya Tope peers across a plain where, in the far distance, a disciplined British-led column with guns and baggage snakes slowly along a road.

dialogue.text: Each time the net tightens, he slips through ravines, dry riverbeds, and the hospitality or silence of local chiefs, some afraid, some sympathetic.

i18n.hi: हर बार जाल कसता है, वह खाइयों, सूखी नदी की धाराओं, और स्थानीय सरदारों की मेहमाननवाज़ी या खामोशी के सहारे फिसल निकलता है—कुछ डरे हुए, कुछ हमदर्द।

panels.shot: intimate mid-shot around a small night council in a ruined temple
panels.action: Inside a ruined stone temple lit by a single lamp, Tatya Tope confers with a few lieutenants, their faces hollowed by exhaustion, while the carved deity looms in shadow behind them.

dialogue.speaker: tatya_tope
dialogue.text: We live so the story of 1857 does not die in one summer.

i18n.hi: हम इसलिए जीते हैं कि १८५७ की कहानी एक ही गर्मी में न मर जाए।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of Tatya Tope walking on foot beside his men and horses
panels.action: In rugged country, Tatya Tope walks on foot beside limping horses and barefoot soldiers, his own sandals worn thin, the line stretching behind him like a frayed rope.

dialogue.text: Letters from British officers mock his ‘ragged rabble’. Yet their own reports note the miles he covers, the months he endures, the small, stinging blows he still can strike.

i18n.hi: अँग्रेज़ अफ़सरों के ख़त उसकी ‘फटी-पुरानी भीड़’ पर हँसते हैं। पर उन्हीं की रपटें उसके तय किए मीलों, झेले महीनों और उसके छोटे, चुभते वारों का हिसाब रखती हैं।

panels.shot: moody mid-shot of Tatya Tope alone by a tree at twilight
panels.action: At twilight, Tatya Tope leans against a gnarled tree on a hillside, looking over darkening fields, his face marked by doubt and stubbornness as distant temple bells ring faintly.

dialogue.text: By early 1859, Sen notes, the rising is broken. Delhi, Lucknow, Jhansi, Gwalior are all under British control. Tatya’s march is no longer for victory, but for memory and honour.

i18n.hi: १८५९ की शुरुआत तक, सेन लिखते हैं, बगावत टूट चुकी थी। दिल्ली, लखनऊ, झाँसी, ग्वालियर सब फिरंगी के हाथ में। तांत्या की यात्रा अब जीत के लिए नहीं, याद और इज़्ज़त के लिए थी।

panels.shot: wide shot of a camp hidden in forest, with a single visitor arriving
panels.action: Deep in a forest clearing, Tatya Tope’s reduced band rests as a lone, well-dressed local chieftain approaches on horseback, his expression guarded, the air heavy with unspoken calculation.

dialogue.text: How he is betrayed, the records tell only from the British side. They speak of a chief who bargains safety with information, and of Tatya trusting a promised shelter.

i18n.hi: उसके साथ ग़द्दारी की कहानी रिकार्ड में सिर्फ़ फिरंगी क़लम से मिलती है। वे बताते हैं एक सरदार का, जिसने अपनी जान के बदले ख़बर बेची, और तांत्या ने जिस पनाह पर भरोसा किया।

panels.shot: tense mid-shot of Tatya Tope being surrounded quietly by loyalist troops
panels.action: Tatya Tope, unarmed and momentarily relaxed, turns in surprise as armed horsemen in the service of a local ruler close in around him, their faces grim, hands firm on their spears.

dialogue.text: Near Paron, in the custody of a Sindhia ally, the long march ends without battle. A rebel general is handed over, bound not by steel, but by politics and fear.

i18n.hi: परों के पास, सिंधिया के साथी की गिरफ़्त में, यह लंबी यात्रा बिना लड़ाई के ख़त्म होती है। बाग़ी सरदार बाँध कर सौंप दिया जाता है, लोहे से नहीं, सियासत और डर से जकड़ा हुआ।

panels.shot: somber interior shot of a colonial courtroom in Shivpuri, with Tatya standing
panels.action: In the small courtroom at Shivpuri in April 1859, Tatya Tope stands straight in simple clothes, surrounded by British officers and scribes, as a military judge listens and soldiers watch.

dialogue.text: At Shivpuri, the proceedings record his name as Tantia Topee. He is charged with rebellion, treason, and the long, stubborn campaign that has defied the Company’s will.

i18n.hi: शिवपुरी में कारवाई के काग़ज़ों में उसका नाम ‘टाँटिया टोपे’ लिखा जाता है। उस पर बगावत, गद्दारी और कंपनी की मर्जी को ठुकरा देने वाली लंबी, ज़िद्दी मुहिम के इल्ज़ाम लगते हैं।

panels.shot: close-up of Tatya Tope’s calm face in court, eyes steady
panels.action: A close view shows Tatya Tope’s calm, weathered face as he looks straight ahead in the courtroom, a faint, almost tired smile at the edge of his mouth.

dialogue.text: British notes record a confession; Indian memory remembers a man unbending. The truth lies between hostile pens and loyal lips, but his composure under sentence is not doubted.

i18n.hi: अँग्रेज़ी नोटों में कबूलनामा दर्ज है; हिंदुस्तानी यादों में एक न झुकने वाला आदमी। सच्चाई दुश्मन क़लमों और वफ़ादार ज़ुबानों के बीच कहीं है, पर सज़ा सुनकर उसकी ठंडक पर कोई शक नहीं।

panels.shot: silhouetted long shot of Tatya Tope being led out at dawn, back to camera
panels.action: At grey dawn outside Shivpuri, Tatya Tope is led away between guards, his back straight, his figure a dark silhouette against the pale sky and faint outline of the town.

dialogue.text: In April 1859, he is executed. British reports close the file on ‘Tantia Topee’, the elusive rebel. But the road he walked is not so easily erased.

i18n.hi: अप्रैल १८५९ में उसे फाँसी दी जाती है। फिरंगी रपटें ‘टाँटिया टोपे’ नाम की फाइल बंद कर देती हैं, उस हाथ न आने वाले बाग़ी की। पर जो राह वह चला, उसे मिटाना इतना आसान नहीं।

panels.shot: wide symbolic shot of an empty road stretching across India’s landscape
panels.action: An empty dirt road winds across a vast Indian landscape—fields, scrub, distant hills—with ghostly, almost imagined impressions of marching feet and hooves fading into the horizon.

dialogue.text: His long march teaches India that even when forts fall and banners are seized, ingenuity and endurance can carry the flame of freedom across dust, years and generations.

i18n.hi: उसकी लंबी यात्रा हिंदुस्तान को सिखाती है कि जब किले गिर जाएँ, झंडे छिन जाएँ, तब भी दिमाग़ और हिम्मत आज़ादी की लौ को धूल, बरसों और पीढ़ियों के बीच ले जा सकती है।

subtitle_i18n.en: The moving flame of the 1857 rising that refused to die
subtitle_i18n.hi: १८५७ की वो चलती ज्वाला, जो बुझने से मना कर गई
